metadata.first_question_preview: In genetic algorithms, representation via integer sequences
metadata.assignment_note: Inferred from segment count/content because translated all-in-one TXT contains no filename headers.

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, representation via integer sequences

answers.b: Is only an implementation exercise, not necessary
answers.c: Is preferable when more than two distinct values are possible for each gene
answers.d: Is not used in genetic algorithms
answers.e: Cannot be used in the case of ordinal attributes

questions.question: In genetic algorithms, representation via permutations

answers.a: Does not allow the use of mutation operators
answers.c: Needs specially defined operators (See Course II: Slide 19)
answers.d: Does not allow the use of recombination operators
answers.e: Does not allow more than two genes with the same value in a chromosome

questions.question: The types of problems that can be solved based on evolutionary computation are: 1. Optimization problems; 2. Search problems in the solution space; 3. Processing of large dimension data (big-data); 4. Modeling problems; 5. Data validation problems; 6. Simulation problems; 7. Dynamic allocation of data in computer memory; 8. Autonomous vehicle navigation

In an evolutionary algorithm, the quality function: 1. Evaluates the quality of the algorithm; 2. Evaluates the quality of each candidate; 3. Evaluates the speed of finding the solution relative to resource consumption; 4. Must be maximized; 5. Determines if a descendant is acceptable; 6. Selects the individuals that will reproduce; 7. Selects the individuals that pass to the next generation; 8. Contains a random factor; 9. Evaluates the quality of the current population; 10. Evaluates the quality of the current population compared to the previous generation.
In genetic algorithms, binary representation

answers.a: Is the most used variant for genotype representation
answers.b: Is not used for genetic algorithms
answers.c: Was the first type of chromosome representation in genetic algorithms (See Course 2: Slide 14)
answers.e: Does not depend on the solved problem

questions.question: The components of evolutionary algorithms are: 1. Representation; 2. Mutation probability; 3. Evaluation function; 4. Recombination probability; 5. Population; 6. Random number generation; 7. Parent selection mechanism; 8. Permutation generation; 9. Graphical representation of quality evolution; 10. Variation operators; 11. Establishment of genetic diversity of the population; 12. Mechanism for replacing the current population; 13. Hillclimbing; 14.Population initialization; 15. Termination condition;
Evolutionary computation is inspired by

answers.e: Biological natural evolution (See Course I: Introduction - Slide 6)

questions.question: The Hillclimbing algorithm: 1. Is applied to a single point in the search space; 2. Application can be repeated for multiple points to increase performance; 3. Is inspired by mountaineering techniques; 4. Always finds the optimal solution; 5. Sometimes finds the optimal solution; 6. Calculations end when the system temperature becomes 0; 7. Usually finds a local optimum point; 8. Is used only for representation with real number strings;

The characteristics of a classic (canonical) genetic algorithm are: 1. Population representation is done via binary strings; 2. Population representation is done via natural number strings; 3. The probability of selecting an individual in the parent multiset is proportional to the value of its fitness function; 4. The probability of selecting an individual in the parent multiset is given by the individual's position in the population hierarchy, determined based on the fitness function; 5. The probability of performing a mutation is small; 6. The probability of performing a mutation is large; 7. The probability of performing recombination is small; 8. The probability of performing recombination is large; 9. Replacement of the current population is done based on age.

answers.a: There is no canonical genetic algorithm

questions.question: Which of the following operators can be used in a genetic algorithm that uses real number string representation: 1. Negation; 2. Fuzzy Negation; 3. Random Resetting; 4. Smoothing/Blurring; 5. Uniform Mutation; 6. Non-uniform Mutation with fixed distribution; 7. Local Mutation; 8. Swap; 9. Insertion; 10. Rapid Mutation; 11. Shuffle; 12. Global Mutation; 13. Inversion; 14. Single-point; 15. Multi-point; 16. Uniform; 17. Root Recombination; 18. Simple Arithmetic; 19. Singular Arithmetic; 20. Total Arithmetic; 21. Maximum String Recombination; 22. PMC; 23. Order Recombination; 24. Edge Recombination; 25. Cycle Recombination
Within an algorithm from the evolutionary strategies class, the mutation operation:

answers.a: Is used only in constrained problems
answers.b: Is performed with a small probability
answers.g: Selects on average half of the individuals for mutation
answers.h: Is performed only once during an algorithm
answers.j: Is performed immediately once an offspring population is available

questions.question: Within a genetic algorithm, the recombination operation:

answers.a: Is performed only once after the first parent selection step
answers.b: Is used with a small probability
answers.c: In general, the recombination probability does not matter in solving problems with genetic algorithms
answers.d: Is performed only once during a genetic algorithm
answers.e: Is performed immediately before every mutation procedure
answers.f: Is performed immediately after every parent selection step
answers.i: Is used only in unconstrained problems

questions.question: Within an evolutionary algorithm, the initial population

answers.a: Is generated at the beginning of every evolutionary cycle
answers.b: Is generated using the normal probability distribution
answers.c: Is generated before the actual evolution begins
answers.d: Is generated after every evolutionary cycle
answers.e: Is generated using the uniform probability distribution
answers.f: Is generated randomly (See Course I: Introduction - Slide 11)
answers.g: Is set to the empty set

questions.question: Given the following two permutation-type chromosomes {6, 3, 11, 7, 14, 8, 5, 15, 1, 2, 4, 13, 9, 10, 12 } and {7, 1, 15, 13, 2, 14, 6, 10, 12, 11, 4, 8, 3, 9, 5}. Applying the PMX recombination operator, with positions 4 and 8, the offspring are obtained.
Within an algorithm from the evolutionary strategies class, the recombination operation:

answers.b: Is performed only once during an algorithm
answers.c: Selects on average half of the individuals for recombination
answers.d: Is performed with a small probability
answers.g: Is performed only once, after the first step of generating a population
answers.h: Determines the obtaining of an offspring multiset in …
answers.i: Is used only in constrained problems

questions.question: Within an algorithm from the evolutionary strategies class, chromosome representation

answers.a: Influences the type of recombination used
answers.b: Is chosen depending on the problem being solved
answers.c: Can only be of integer or real number string type
answers.d: Can be any of: binary strings, integer strings, real number strings, permutations
answers.e: Influences the mechanism for selecting the next generation
answers.f: Does not influence the type of mutation used (discrete/ non-discrete)
answers.g: Can only be of real number strings
answers.h: Contains both the description of the candidate individual and the parameters that ..

questions.question: Within a genetic algorithm, the parent selection operation:

answers.a: Is always based on a selection probability distribution
answers.c: Is performed only once during a genetic algorithm
answers.d: Is used only in constrained problems
answers.e: Is performed immediately before every mutation procedure
answers.f: Is performed immediately once an evaluated current population is available
answers.g: Is performed only once after the first step of generating a population
answers.h: Generally selects individuals based on the age factor
answers.l: Can be realized by using a selection probability distribution

questions.question: Given the permutation-type chromosome {7, 6, 12, 14, 3, 10, 8, 15, 11, 5, 4, 1, 13, 2, 9} Following the application of the shuffle mutation operator, the chromosome {7, 14, 13, 12, 1, 15, 2, 8, 6, 3, 11, 5, 10, 4, 9 } was obtained.The two positions used for the shuffle are:
Within a genetic algorithm, the mutation operation:

answers.b: Is applied only if genetic diversity drops below a given threshold x
answers.d: Is applied to the offspring produced by the recombination operation
answers.e: Is applied to the parent multiset
answers.f: Is applied to the current population
answers.g: Is used only in constrained problems
answers.i: Is performed only once, after the initial population generation
answers.j: Is applied immediately before every next generation selection step
answers.k: Is used only in unconstrained problems

questions.question: Within a genetic algorithm, the survivor selection operation

answers.b: In some variants requires the calculation of a selection probability distribution
answers.c: The chosen individuals are always feasible
answers.d: Leads to an increase in the average quality of the current population
answers.e: Is applied at the beginning of every iteration
answers.g: Is applied to the current population
answers.h: Guarantees obtaining a generation with superior average quality if it uses age-based selection.
answers.i: Ensures the perpetuation of the individual with maximum quality from the current population
answers.j: Chooses the next generation from the individuals available after the mutation operation
answers.k: Is applied to the descendants obtained from the current population

questions.correct_raw: b, d, f, g, i, j, k
